# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS volumes for our application data. Focus on getting the instances running quickly. We'll add encryption and backup policies in phase 2 after we validate the architecture.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: EC2 instances with attached EBS volumes for application data

Parameters:
  VpcId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC::Id
    Description: VPC where instances will run

  SubnetId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet::Id
    Description: Subnet for the EC2 instance

  InstanceType:
    Type: String
    Default: t3.micro
    AllowedValues:
      - t3.micro
      - t3.small
      - t3.medium
      - t3.large
      - m5.large
      - m5.xlarge
    Description: EC2 instance type

  KeyName:
    Type: AWS::EC2::KeyPair::KeyName
    Description: Optional EC2 key pair name for SSH access

  ImageId:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Image::Id
    Description: AMI ID for the instance

  VolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 100
    MinValue: 8
    Description: Size of the application data EBS volume in GiB

  VolumeType:
    Type: String
    Default: gp3
    AllowedValues:
      - gp3
      - gp2
      - io1
      - st1
      - sc1
    Description: EBS volume type for application data

  RootVolumeSize:
    Type: Number
    Default: 20
    MinValue: 8
    Description: Root volume size in GiB

  SSHLocation:
    Type: String
    Default: 0.0.0.0/0
    Description: CIDR block allowed to access SSH

  InstanceCount:
    Type: Number
    Default: 1
    MinValue: 1
    MaxValue: 10
    Description: Number of EC2 instances to launch

  AppPort:
    Type: Number
    Default: 8080
    Description: Application port to allow inbound traffic

  DataDeviceName:
    Type: String
    Default: /dev/sdf
    Description: Device name for the application data volume

Mappings:
  RegionMap:
    us-east-1:
      HVM64: ami-0c02fb55956c7d316
    us-east-2:
      HVM64: ami-0f30f9c1a7f1f3f6a
    us-west-1:
      HVM64: ami-0d6621c01e8c2de2c
    us-west-2:
      HVM64: ami-0892d3c7ee96c0bf7

Resources:
  InstanceSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Security group for application EC2 instances
      VpcId: !Ref VpcId
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: !Ref AppPort
          ToPort: !Ref AppPort
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 22
          ToPort: 22
          CidrIp: !Ref SSHLocation
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: -1
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0

  InstanceRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore

  InstanceProfile:
    Type: AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile
    Properties:
      Roles:
        - !Ref InstanceRole

  ApplicationLaunchTemplate:
    Type: AWS::EC2::LaunchTemplate
    Properties:
      LaunchTemplateData:
        ImageId: !Ref ImageId
        InstanceType: !Ref InstanceType
        KeyName: !Ref KeyName
        SecurityGroupIds:
          - !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup
        IamInstanceProfile:
          Arn: !GetAtt InstanceProfile.Arn
        BlockDeviceMappings:
          - DeviceName: /dev/xvda
            Ebs:
              VolumeSize: !Ref RootVolumeSize
              VolumeType: gp3
              DeleteOnTermination: true
              Encrypted: false
          - DeviceName: !Ref DataDeviceName
            Ebs:
              VolumeSize: !Ref VolumeSize
              VolumeType: !Ref VolumeType
              DeleteOnTermination: true
              Encrypted: false
        UserData:
          Fn::Base64: !Sub |
            #!/bin/bash -xe
            yum update -y || true
            mkfs -t xfs /dev/xvdf || true
            mkdir -p /data
            grep -q '/data' /etc/fstab || echo '/dev/xvdf /data xfs defaults,nofail 0 2' >> /etc/fstab
            mount -a || true
            chmod 777 /data
            echo "Instance initialized" > /data/instance.txt

  ApplicationASG:
    Type: AWS::AutoScaling::AutoScalingGroup
    Properties:
      VPCZoneIdentifier:
        - !Ref SubnetId
      MinSize: !Ref InstanceCount
      MaxSize: !Ref InstanceCount
      DesiredCapacity: !Ref InstanceCount
      LaunchTemplate:
        LaunchTemplateId: !Ref ApplicationLaunchTemplate
        Version: !GetAtt ApplicationLaunchTemplate.LatestVersionNumber
      HealthCheckType: EC2
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: !Sub ${AWS::StackName}-app
          PropagateAtLaunch: true

Outputs:
  SecurityGroupId:
    Description: Security group ID
    Value: !Ref InstanceSecurityGroup

  LaunchTemplateId:
    Description: Launch template ID
    Value: !Ref ApplicationLaunchTemplate

  AutoScalingGroupName:
    Description: Auto Scaling group name
    Value: !Ref ApplicationASG
